articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The investment signals growing institutional interest in FEC-focused fintech infrastructure. For operators, this could mean expanded capabilities and wider adoption of Amusement Connect's payment and management tools across venues.

articles.title: How AI Is Already Transforming FEC Operations — And How to Leverage It
articles.summary: Modern venue management platforms are quietly embedding AI into everyday FEC operations, from transaction analysis and game performance tracking to party booking optimization and card reload patterns. Operators who tap into this existing data layer can unlock meaningful revenue and efficiency gains. The article argues that most FEC operators are already using AI without realizing it, and explains how to be more intentional about it.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: Venue management software ROLLER has partnered with global payments platform Adyen to provide fast, embedded financing through a Capital product available to more than 3,000 venues on its platform. This gives FEC operators a streamlined path to growth capital without going through traditional lenders. The integration represents a significant expansion of financial services within the FEC software ecosystem.

articles.title: Sacoa Installs Cashless Technology at Virginia Pinstack Location
articles.summary: Sacoa has completed a cashless payment system installation at a Pinstack family entertainment center in Virginia, continuing the industry's broader shift away from tokens and cash. Cashless and card-based systems are now table stakes for competitive FECs, streamlining guest spending and improving revenue tracking. This deployment highlights Sacoa's continued presence in the multi-attraction FEC segment.

articles.title: Six Flags Expands Flexible Membership Program to Six Additional Parks
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation is rolling out its Flexible Membership program to six more parks, giving guests a value-driven, multi-park pass option with tiered access. The move reflects broader industry trends toward subscription and membership models that drive recurring revenue and loyalty. FEC operators should take note of how large regional chains are engineering guest retention through flexible pass structures.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Center in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track — a format that maximizes entertainment square footage in constrained urban footprints. The opening continues K1 Speed's national expansion of premium indoor karting venues. Multi-level track design is an increasingly relevant concept for FEC developers looking to differentiate in competitive markets.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Expands into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Venue
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is continuing its aggressive nationwide franchise growth with a new location in Maryland, underscoring the brand's strategy of bringing large-format indoor adventure parks to suburban markets. The expansion highlights ongoing demand for trampoline and adventure park concepts in the mid-Atlantic region. Franchise operators and competitors should monitor Urban Air's site selection and format as a benchmark.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Previews New Salem, Oregon Location
articles.summary: A sneak peek at Urban Air's upcoming Salem, Oregon location offers a look at the brand's standard attraction mix as it pushes into the Pacific Northwest. The opening adds another data point to Urban Air's continued franchise expansion across diverse regional markets. Local FEC competitors should assess the competitive impact on their markets as the brand grows its footprint.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Asmodee to Launch Spot It! Summer Experience
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has teamed with game publisher Asmodee to introduce a Spot It!-themed summer experience across its locations, blending physical card game IP with the FEC environment. Brand partnerships like this offer FECs a cost-effective way to create limited-time programming that drives repeat visits during peak summer season. The collaboration is a useful model for smaller FEC operators looking to activate IP partnerships.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Closes After Two Years in Operation
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has permanently closed its doors just two years after opening, serving as a cautionary tale about the challenges of sustaining new FEC ventures in competitive or under-resourced markets. The closure comes during a period when several FECs are simultaneously opening and failing across the country. Operators and investors should examine the factors — location, concept, capitalization — that contribute to early-stage FEC failures.

articles.title: Play CENLA FEC in Louisiana Shuts Down
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment center in Rapides Parish, Louisiana, has closed its doors, adding to a recent string of FEC closures across the country. The shutdown highlights the ongoing operational and financial pressures facing independent FEC operators post-pandemic. Industry stakeholders should pay attention to the patterns behind these closures to better understand what business models are struggling.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Takes Lease in Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease to occupy part of the former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the trend of FEC concepts backfilling dead retail space in struggling malls. This adaptive reuse model has become a key growth vehicle for entertainment operators seeking affordable, high-footprint real estate. The deal is a strong example of the symbiotic relationship between entertainment tenants and mall redevelopment.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Advances Development at Southside Commons in Jacksonville
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is moving forward with its development at the Southside Commons mixed-use project in Jacksonville, Florida, signaling continued growth in the adventure park segment. The project represents the integration of FEC attractions into larger retail and lifestyle developments. Operators and developers should monitor this mixed-use model as a blueprint for anchoring entertainment in new commercial districts.

articles.title: Madison, Mississippi Entertainment District 'The Gallery' to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi this summer, bringing a multi-concept leisure destination to the market. Mixed-use entertainment districts continue to emerge as a popular development format that benefits anchor FEC tenants through shared foot traffic. The project is worth watching as a case study in entertainment-led real estate development in mid-size markets.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Large-Scale Themed Entertainment
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, Wisconsin, anchored by a large fire-breathing dragon centerpiece, bringing a new theming-forward hospitality and entertainment concept to one of the Midwest's top family vacation markets. The venue blends resort accommodations with immersive entertainment, a format gaining traction among investors seeking to expand guest spend per visit. FEC operators can look to this model for inspiration on experiential differentiation and theming investment.

articles.title: Southern California Birthday Party Market Study Points to Strong FEC Demand
articles.summary: A new report highlights that Southern California families are actively searching for birthday party venues, underscoring the continued importance of party programming as a revenue driver for FECs. Birthday events remain one of the highest-margin, most loyalty-building occasions in the FEC business model. Operators in competitive urban markets should assess whether their party packages are positioned to capture this demand.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Invests in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has invested in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform serving the amusement and FEC industry. The investment reinforces growing private equity interest in technology infrastructure built specifically for amusement operators. This is a companion report to the Business Wire announcement and provides additional investor-focused context.

articles.title: Europa-Park Named Fastest-Growing Non-Disney/Non-Universal Park in Western Hemisphere
articles.summary: A Themed Entertainment Association study highlighted by Forbes identifies Europa-Park in Germany as the fastest-growing independent theme park in the Western hemisphere based on visitor growth from 2005 to 2024. The finding offers inspiration for independent park operators competing against major chains, demonstrating that focused investment and brand identity can drive sustained long-term growth. FEC investors should take note of the business model traits that have driven Europa-Park's remarkable performance.

articles.title: New Bear River Entertainment Zone Draws Interest from Local Business Community
articles.summary: A new entertainment zone at Bear River has attracted attention from chamber members in Fortuna and Eureka, California, indicating local business optimism about the venue's economic impact. Destination entertainment zones that draw community backing often benefit from referral networks and co-marketing with neighboring businesses. The story is a useful example of how FEC operators can build goodwill and visibility through community engagement.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Summer Program Returns to Kentucky and Southern Indiana Venues
articles.summary: Bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary lanes to children to drive family traffic during the off-peak season. The program is a proven marketing tool for bowling FECs to build summer attendance and introduce new families to their venues. Operators not yet enrolled in Kids Bowl Free should evaluate it as a low-cost summer traffic driver.

articles.title: dvLED vs. Projection: A Practical Guide for Attraction Operators
articles.summary: A detailed technical breakdown examines the real trade-offs between direct-view LED and projection display systems for entertainment and attraction environments, covering contrast, geometry, acoustics, and total cost of ownership. The analysis is authored by an experienced AV technologist and cuts through marketing hype to give operators actionable guidance. FEC and attraction operators investing in immersive display technology should read this before making a purchasing decision.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Bowling Alley Permanently Closes
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed after years of operation, with management directing bowlers to a nearby Appleton location. The closure is part of a broader pattern of standalone bowling alleys struggling to compete with full-service FEC formats and rising operating costs. Independent bowling operators should monitor these closures as a signal to evaluate diversification of their entertainment and food-and-beverage offerings.

articles.title: London-Based Interactive Entertainment Concept with Virtual Soccer and Arcade Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London entertainment venue featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and casual dining is set to open a Paris location, illustrating how adult-oriented interactive entertainment concepts are expanding across European urban markets. The format — blending competitive socializing, tech-driven games, and F&B — mirrors trends already well established in the U.S. FEC market. Operators can look to this cross-Atlantic expansion as validation of the social entertainment concept's staying power.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Adds New Parade and Summer Events for 2026
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure is enhancing its summer 2026 programming with a new twice-daily parade, seasonal festivals, wildlife encounters, and the opening of Shoreline Pier at The Boardwalk. The additions reflect the park's strategy of layering live entertainment and events on top of its rides to extend dwell time and repeat visitation. FEC operators can draw lessons from this event-layering approach to increase guest spend and frequency.

articles.title: Enchanted Forest Water Safari Opens 70th Anniversary Season with New Attractions
articles.summary: Enchanted Forest Water Safari, New York's largest water theme park, is kicking off its 70th anniversary season with new attractions including Paul Bunyan's Log Haul. The park's longevity and continued reinvestment offer a compelling case study in sustaining a family attraction across generations. Independent FEC operators can take inspiration from how heritage venues use anniversary milestones to drive media coverage and renewed guest interest.

articles.title: Dorney Park Hosts 4,600 Children for Annual Community Day of Giving
articles.summary: Dorney Park & Wildwater Kingdom donated more than 4,600 complimentary admissions to children and families served by local nonprofits as part of its annual Day of Giving program. Community give-back initiatives like this generate significant goodwill, positive media coverage, and lasting brand loyalty in local markets. FEC operators of all sizes can adapt this model to strengthen community ties and differentiate from competitors.

articles.title: The GAB Spot in Fort Walton Beach Builds Strong Local Following
articles.summary: The GAB Spot, a family entertainment venue in Fort Walton Beach, Florida, is attracting a loyal local customer base, demonstrating the viability of community-focused FEC concepts in mid-size coastal markets. The venue's grassroots success highlights how strong local marketing and a welcoming atmosphere can drive repeat visitation without major brand recognition. Independent FEC operators can look to this example for community engagement strategies.
